prompt: Share an untranslatable word or idiom from any language you know about — something that doesn't have a clean English equivalent — and explain what it captures.
response:
Kilig – Tagalog (Philippines)

Strictly speaking, kilig is an adjective, but Filipinos use it almost like a noun or even a verb (“Kinikilig ako!”).  
It names the bubbly, light-headed rush you feel when something romantic happens to you—not full-blown love, not simple happiness, but that sudden, fizzy internal fireworks: your crush says your name, your favorite K-drama leads finally kiss, or someone brushes your hand on purpose. English can paraphrase it as “giddy” or “thrilled,” but those words miss the sweet, fluttery, exclusively romantic voltage that kilig carries.
